pattern: Dijkstra from the origin node computes remaining move budget at each original node; the inserted nodes reachable along each edge are bounded by the budget at each endpoint, capped at the edge's inserted count.
hints: Start by figuring out how many moves remain when you reach each original node — this is a shortest-path problem from node 0.

Once you know the remaining budget at each endpoint of an edge, the inserted nodes you can reach from the left end plus those from the right end together cover at most all inserted nodes on that edge.
Sum the original nodes whose shortest distance from 0 does not exceed maxMoves, then for every edge add min(dist[u], cnt) + min(dist[v], cnt) capped at cnt to count the reachable inserted nodes.

edge_inputs.description: three-node graph where traversing subdivided edges from node 0 reaches thirteen total nodes
four-node graph with enough budget to cover almost all inserted nodes
node 0 is isolated so only it is reachable regardless of budget
single node no edges only the start node is reachable
single node with very large budget still just one node
zero moves means only node 0 itself is reachable
edges with zero inserted nodes each crossing costs exactly one move
two paths to same destination shorter path allows more inserted-node coverage on the far edge
budget exactly reaches an original node leaving zero spare for inserted nodes on adjacent edges

stress.description: sparse random graph with varying inserted-node counts and a moderate move budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 300))
    seen = set()
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, min(n, 30)):
        u = rng.randint(0, v - 1)
        key = (min(u, v), max(u, v))
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            cnt = rng.randint(0, 50)
            edges.append([u, v, cnt])
    extra = rng.randint(0, min(20, n))
    attempts = 0
    while extra > 0 and attempts < extra * 10:
        attempts += 1
        u = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        v = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if u == v:
            continue
        key = (min(u, v), max(u, v))
        if key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(key)
        cnt = rng.randint(0, 100)
        edges.append([u, v, cnt])
        extra -= 1
    maxMoves = rng.randint(0, 1000)
    return [edges, maxMoves, n]
stress.note: random sparse graphs with various budgets to stress Dijkstra and the cap formula
